basics.name: Kanha Batra
basics.label: Postdoctoral Fellow in Computational Neuroscience & AI

work.name: Stanford University
work.summary: Jointly advised by Lisa Giocomo (Giocomo Lab) and Scott Linderman (Linderman Lab). Research focuses on modeling hippocampal remapping as a computational framework for continual learning in AI.
highlights: Modeling hippocampal remapping for non-stationary representation learning
Applying state-space models to large-scale neural population recordings
Developing neuro-inspired architectures for continual learning in AI

work.name: Amazon Science
work.summary: Seller Partner Recruitment & Success Team. Built model explanation and attribution pipelines for high-impact event identification.
highlights: Built a KernelSHAP-based model explanation pipeline for multi-class classification problems
Implemented TimeSHAP for event attribution in recurrent neural network models

work.name: Salk Institute for Biological Studies / UC San Diego
work.summary: Systems Neuroscience Lab, advised by Kay Tye (Salk Institute) and Terrence Sejnowski (Salk / UCSD). Dissertation work combined probabilistic modeling and deep reinforcement learning to study prefrontal cortex computation in social behavior and flexible cognition.
highlights: Developed HMM-GLM framework to model mPFC internal states and predict behavioral motifs during social competition (ROC-AUC ~0.92)
Devised novel Multi-Context Foraging Task for contextual decision-making in rodents and RL simulations
Developed Neuromodulated Mixture of Experts architecture for lifelong learning, inspired by PFC neuromodulation

Publications: Cortical ensembles orchestrate social competition (Nature, 2022) · Neurotensin orchestrates valence assignment (Nature, 2022) · Thalamus sends arousal but not valence to amygdala (Psychopharmacology, 2023) · Predicting stress-induced anhedonia from cortical dynamics (bioRxiv, 2024) · Social isolation escalates alcohol drinking via amygdala-cortical circuitry (bioRxiv, 2024)

work.name: UC San Diego — Multimodal Imaging Lab
work.summary: Advised by Tyler Seibert and Anders Dale. Medical imaging ML for prostate cancer and bone metastasis segmentation.
highlights: Generated annotations for malignant lesions in diffusion MRI across 50+ prostate cancer patients
Developed multi-compartment classifier for automatic segmentation of bone-metastasised cancer cells

work.name: Centre for Advanced Research in Imaging, Neuroscience & Genomics
work.summary: Mahajan Imaging. Developed a pipeline for 3D mesh reconstruction of coronary anatomy from chest CT scans and computed Fractional Flow Reserve for non-invasive stenosis quantification.

work.name: IIT Delhi — Medical Image Processing Lab
work.summary: Advisor: Amit Mehndiratta. EMG signal acquisition and processing via robotic exoskeleton for neuro-muscular rehabilitation in stroke patients.

work.name: ETH Zürich — deMello Group
work.summary: Advisor: Casadevall i Solvas. Microfluidics & Nanoscience Lab. Automated valve control and real-time cell tracking for high-throughput cell division experiments.

education.area: Machine Learning & Data Science (Electrical & Computer Engineering)
courses: Statistical Learning for Neural Signal Processing
highlights: Advisors: Kay Tye (Salk Institute) & Terrence Sejnowski (Salk / UCSD)
Dissertation: Computational models of prefrontal cortex for social behavior and flexible cognition

education.area: Machine Learning & Data Science (Electrical & Computer Engineering)
highlights: MS program fully funded by research position in Multimodal Imaging Lab

interests.name: Research Interests
keywords: Recurrent neural networks as brain models

volunteer.summary: Mentored a high school student in computational neuroscience research through Salk's High School Scholars program.

volunteer.organization: Salk Institute / March of Dimes High School Science Week
volunteer.summary: Panelist representing the Systems Neuroscience Lab for Salk's Virtual March of Dimes High School Science Week.
Core team at India's largest student-run NGO tackling blood shortages.
Contributed to improving Wikipedia's coverage of women in science.
